name: Radar.io Location Platform API
what_it_does: Location platform with REST API — enabling mobile apps and IoT products to implement geofencing, location context (place detection), trip tracking, geocoding, and map rendering, as a developer-friendly and cost-effective alternative to Google Maps Platform or Foursquare, with SDKs for real-time location tracking and event-driven geofence triggers.
use_cases: Implementing geofencing triggers via Radar API for location-based app notifications and automation
Geocoding and reverse geocoding addresses via Radar API for address validation in mobile apps
Detecting user location context (home, work, store) via Radar Places API for personalization
Tracking trip progress and ETAs via Radar API for delivery and rideshare applications
Building map-based features via Radar Maps API for cost-effective alternative to Google Maps
not_for: Desktop-only applications (Radar is mobile-first with iOS and Android SDKs)
Teams needing only routing without location context (use GraphHopper or ORS for pure routing)
High-precision centimeter-level location (Radar uses GPS/cell/WiFi — not RTK GPS)
best_when: A mobile app developer needs geofencing, location context, and geocoding at a fraction of Google Maps pricing — with developer-friendly SDKs, events webhooks, and a generous free tier for startups.
avoid_when: You need centimeter-level precision, complex turn-by-turn navigation, or desktop-only applications.

auth.notes: Publishable key for client-side SDK (safe for mobile apps). Secret key for server-side API (must be server-only). Two key types: Test keys (development) and Live keys (production). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhook for geofence enter/exit events, trip events, insights events. SDKs for iOS, Android, JavaScript, React Native, Flutter, Cordova. Key management in Radar dashboard.

pricing.paid_tiers: Developer $49/month (500K calls); Growth $149/month (2M calls); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2016 by Nick Patrick and Jeff Eiden. Raised $55M Series B (2022, Accel, Recruit Strategic Partners). Google Maps Platform alternative at 10x lower cost positioning. 100,000 free API calls/month (generous for startups). Used by: Walmart, T-Mobile, Home Depot, Uber Eats for location features. Geofencing: 1000 geofences on free, unlimited on paid. Place detection (Starbucks, Whole Foods, airport, etc.). Trip tracking for delivery ETA. Radar Maps: street maps without Google attribution. Strong developer experience (docs, SDKs). SOC2 certified.

performance.rate_limits: 100K free/month; varies by plan; no burst rate documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API + mobile SDKs. Publishable + secret key pair. Webhook for geofence and trip events. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. 100K free/month. Test/live key environments. Excellent developer documentation. Mobile-first design — server API complements mobile SDK.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Geocoding requests fully idempotent. Geofence reads idempotent by ID. User reads idempotent by userId. Event history reads idempotent. Track calls idempotent for same location.
known_agent_gotchas: Two key types: publishable (client-safe) vs secret (server-only) — NEVER expose secret key in mobile app
Test vs Live environments use different keys — don't mix environments
Geofencing triggers via mobile SDK — server API for management, not real-time mobile tracking
100K free calls/month — includes all API calls (geocoding, tracking, geofence management)
Mobile SDK required for real-time background location tracking — pure server API has limitations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Publishable vs secret key separation (good security design). Secret key server-only — reduces mobile app key exposure risk. Test/live environment isolation. Location data (GPS) is sensitive PII — GDPR data minimization required. US and EU data residency. Webhook signature validation available. SOC2 Type II certification.
